data_in.description: Sparse pairwise distance matrix from nearest neighbor index construction

data_out.description: Cluster assignment file mapping spectrum identifiers to cluster labels

domain_knowledge: Density-based clustering (e.g., DBSCAN) groups spectra by local density rather than global distance, making it suitable for spectra of varying cluster sizes and irregular shapes.
A sparse pairwise distance matrix contains only non-zero distances for nearest neighbor pairs, reducing memory and computation compared to a full distance matrix.
Cluster assignment output must preserve spectrum identifiers to enable downstream interpretation and cross-referencing with the original spectral data.
MS/MS spectra similarity is typically measured using cosine distance or spectral angle after binning and feature hashing normalization.

direct_checks: verify that the cluster assignment file exists and is readable
verify file_format_is cluster assignment output (e.g. TSV, CSV, or JSON with spectrum identifiers and cluster labels)
verify field_present: cluster assignment file contains at least two columns/fields: spectrum identifier and cluster label
verify row_count_equals: cluster assignment file contains one row per input spectrum (row count matches input spectrum count)
verify contains_substring: cluster assignment file contains valid cluster identifiers (numeric or alphanumeric labels, no nulls or 'None' values for assigned spectra)
verify script_runs: density-based clustering implementation (DBSCAN or equivalent) executes without errors on sparse distance matrix input
verify output_matches_reference: cluster assignments are reproducible (byte-for-byte or robust to parameter choices, depending on implementation determinism)
expert_review: assess whether cluster assignments are biologically/chemically meaningful by comparing against known spectrum families or reference spectral libraries, if available
assess whether the density threshold parameters (e.g. eps, min_samples for DBSCAN) are appropriately justified and documented
assess whether cluster quality metrics (silhouette score, Davies-Bouldin index, or domain-specific measures) meet expected thresholds for mass spectrometry data

cmd: import sys; from spectrum_utils.clustering import dbscan_clustering; import pickle; dm = pickle.load(open('{inputs.distance_matrix}', 'rb')); clusters = dbscan_clustering(dm, eps=0.1, min_samples=1); output = open('cluster_assignments.tsv', 'w'); [output.write(f'{i}\t{c}\n') for i, c in enumerate(clusters)]; output.close()

task_description: Perform density-based clustering on a sparse pairwise distance matrix of MS/MS spectra to assign each spectrum to a cluster. Output a cluster assignment file mapping spectrum identifiers to cluster labels.
task_objective: Implement density-based clustering on a sparse pairwise distance matrix to assign spectra to clusters and output cluster assignments.
title: Reconstruct the Density-Based Clustering Layer of the falcon pipeline
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load the sparse pairwise distance matrix (output from nearest neighbor indexing step) in a format compatible with density-based clustering algorithms. 2. Apply density-based clustering (such as DBSCAN or equivalent) to the distance matrix to identify spectrum clusters based on local density and neighborhood connectivity. 3. Assign each spectrum a cluster label and generate a cluster assignment table with spectrum identifiers and corresponding cluster membership. 4. Save cluster assignments to a structured output file (CSV or equivalent tabular format).
